Dear Blanca:
this is to request your advice about a question concerning
mass rnaiLings by elected officials. 
I would appreciate your
response in writing.
At specific issue here is a program established by the State
Treasurer called the college Savings Bond Program. Under
this program, we are trying to get citizens to buy certain
bonds which can be redeemed in the future to be used for
college expenses.
To operate the progran, we must advertise it in order to
"so11cit" inquires about the progran from potential
investors. The advertisenents will only occur in general
circutation newspapers such as the Los Angeles Times. our
assumption is that only subscribers wouLd receive the
newsplper, however, sorne copies may be given to schools
and/or cther groups despite our intent.
My specific question is rrpursuant to the mass mailingf
plovisions of the Political Reform Act, what, if any,
iestrictions would be placed on the content of these
advertisements?rr
Sincerely,
bt&,
HAL GEIOGUE
Assistant Treasurer

sections.question: Pursuant to the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act, what, if any, restrictions would be placed on the content of these advertisements?
sections.question_synthetic: What restrictions, if any, does the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions place on advertisements for the State Treasurer's College Savings Bond Program?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The question is clearly stated. Document appears to be from the Office of the Treasurer requesting guidance before the FPPC has responded.

embedding.summary: Assistant Treasurer Hal Geiogue requests FPPC advice regarding mass mailing restrictions under the Political Reform Act as applied to advertisements for the State Treasurer's College Savings Bond Program to be published in general circulation newspapers.
